content:
Aging With AI
It just takes a decision

6 min read
Just now

--

Press enter or click to view image in full size

Photo by Sean Thomas on UnsplashOptimism is not an inherent character trait. It is a skill that can be learned at any age.
Science has proven that the idea that we’re too old to learn — the pessimist’s lament — is just an idea, just negative self-talk. It’s not a reality, and it can be changed.
If you’ve found yourself thinking, “I’m too old for this,” or “I’ll never understand AI,” just consider for a minute the possibility that it’s just not true.
(And if you’ve found yourself thinking that way about the ability of an older loved one, it’s important for you to consider as well.)
You might have experienced frustrations with new technologies in the past. Many of us have.
But now we know that the idea that we’re not capable of learning is just a pattern of thinking, and patterns can change.
You can change yours.
That’s now been proven by decades of research, which began with the work of a psychologist named Martin Seligman, author of the wonderful book Learned Optimism.
Optimism Is a Skill, Not a Personality Trait
